summary: Overall UK risk remains Elevated. The key system-shaping drivers are external: the Bank of England continues to frame the Middle East conflict as a material energy/commodity supply shock that raises near-term UK CPI inflation risk and can weaken activity, while the Russia–Ukraine war remains active with continued high-intensity long-range strike activity even amid short, tactical ceasefire announcements. Domestically, disruption risk remains concentrated in public services (resident-doctor industrial action ending this morning after a week of strain) and in cyber exposure for widely deployed edge infrastructure products where UK organisations are being urged to remediate quickly.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: UK inflation remains above target and the Bank of England continues to highlight conflict-driven energy and commodity price pressures as an upside risk to near-term CPI and a drag on activity.
drivers: ONS: CPI rose 3.0% y/y in February 2026 (unchanged vs January), keeping inflation above target. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

Bank of England: MPC held Bank Rate at 3.75% (March 2026) and warned Middle East conflict-driven rises in global energy/commodity prices will raise near-term CPI inflation. https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy-summary-and-minutes/2026/march-2026
Brent crude remains a key pass-through channel for UK fuel and utility costs

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: The UK terrorism baseline remains SUBSTANTIAL and major conflict risks persist, with Russia continuing large-scale drone and missile activity against Ukraine and Middle East tensions sustaining shipping and energy-security uncertainty.
drivers: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels
BBC reports continued large-scale Russian drone/missile strikes on Ukraine amid ‘Easter escalation’ framing. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cew70wjvv9do
Gulf and Red Sea shipping insecurity remains a material spillover risk for trade and energy

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: Government crisis-management activity remains focused on economic and domestic impacts of the Middle East conflict; no evidence reviewed indicates an acute UK institutional instability event.
drivers: GOV.UK: COBR(M) meeting on 23 March 2026 focused on domestic economic impacts and anti-profiteering measures. https://www.gov.uk/government/news/readout-cobrm-iran-economic-and-domestic-impacts-23-march-2026
Legislative workload continues with expected ministerial statements on Middle East and security issues. https://www.hansardsociety.org.uk/news/parliament-matters-bulletin-13-april-2026
Policy bandwidth is being consumed by cost-of-living, energy-security, and public-service resilience pressures

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: The recent Easter-weekend storm cycle has cleared, but the Met Office signals continued changeable conditions; near-term hazard posture is best treated as watchful rather than acute.
drivers: Met Office: Storm Dave affected the UK during 4–5 April 2026 with disruptive wind/rain/snow impacts in northern and western areas. https://www.metoffice.gov.uk/blog/2026/storm-dave-in-context

Met Office: guidance continues to emphasise unsettled, at-times windy conditions over the following days (10-day outlook). https://www.metoffice.gov.uk/blog/2026/met-office-weather-forecast-for-the-next-10-days-april-1
Flood and travel disruption risk can re-emerge quickly in locally heavy rainfall; rely on official warnings for time-critical updates

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: NHS operational strain from resident-doctor strike action remains a recent disruption driver, but the strike window ends this morning, reducing immediate pressure risk even as recovery backlogs persist.
drivers: NHS England: resident-doctor industrial action scheduled to end at 6:59am on Monday 13 April 2026. https://www.england.nhs.uk/2026/04/public-reminded-to-access-care-as-normal-this-weekend-as-nhs-staff-weather-the-storm-of-doctors-strike/
NHS England planning guidance notes that this round is challenging due to Easter timing and staffing strain. https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/industrial-action-bma-resident-doctors-april-2026-2/

London Underground strike dates later in April could drive local transport disruption. https://www.timeout.com/london/news/london-tube-and-train-strikes-spring-2026-full-list-of-dates-and-lines-impacted-how-to-travel-everything-you-need-to-know-march-april-may-031026

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: The advisory environment remains elevated: UK organisations are being urged to remediate widely used edge access infrastructure (notably Citrix NetScaler), and exploitation pressure against internet-facing perimeter systems remains persistent.
drivers: NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3055, CVE-2026-4368). https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/ncsc-urges-immediate-action-to-mitigate-citrix-netscaler-vulnerabilities

Rapid7: CVE-2026-3055 advisory and remediation guidance for affected NetScaler versions. https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-cve-2026-3055-citrix-netscaler-adc-and-netscaler-gateway-out-of-bounds-read/
Edge/perimeter exploitation remains a common initial-access pathway for ransomware and intrusion activity

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent at 102.15 USD/bbl (as of April 13, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (y/y)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

indicators.name: Bank Rate (UK)
indicators.note: Bank of England: MPC maintained Bank Rate at 3.75% (March 2026). https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy-summary-and-minutes/2026/march-2026

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: Resident doctor industrial action (England)
indicators.note: NHS England: latest round of resident-doctor industrial action ends at 6:59am on Monday 13 April 2026. https://www.england.nhs.uk/2026/04/public-reminded-to-access-care-as-normal-this-weekend-as-nhs-staff-weather-the-storm-of-doctors-strike/

changes_since_last_update: Brent crude indicator updated to 102.15 USD/bbl using Trading Economics (as of April 13, 2026).
Natural Hazards trend set to improving following clearance of the early-April storm cycle, while remaining Guarded.
Social Stability trend set to improving as resident-doctor industrial action ends this morning; status remains Elevated due to recovery and disruption tail effects.
priority_watch_items: Energy spillovers: monitor for renewed upward pressure on wholesale energy and fuel prices and associated UK inflation re-acceleration.
Geopolitical spillovers: monitor Middle East and Russia–Ukraine developments that could disrupt shipping routes, insurance, or commodity supply.
NHS resilience: track post-strike recovery, elective backlogs, and any escalation to critical incidents/OPEL pressures.
Edge-vulnerability exposure: confirm patch/mitigation for Citrix NetScaler and watch for exploitation signals.
Severe weather: monitor official flood and weather warnings for fast-changing local disruptions.
source_notes: This update prioritises official UK sources (BoE, ONS, MI5, NHS England, NCSC) and the Met Office, plus Trading Economics for a Brent price snapshot.
Some indicators are published on schedules (ONS/BoE) or reflect a baseline (terrorism threat level), so they are not real-time signals.
Cyber status reflects elevated advisory and exposure context rather than confirmed UK national-scale outages.
